content.problemStatement:
Beware the ides of March.

Julius Caesar was warned by a soothsayer to be wary of the ides of March — namely, the 151515-th of March.

Today is the NNN-th day of March. Your task is to tell Caesar whether it is the ides of March, so that he can take extra safety precautions if necessary.


Input Format
The only line of input will contain a single integer NNN, today's date in March.



Output Format
Print `"Yes"` if today is the ides of March, and `"No"` otherwise (without quotes).


Each letter of the output may be printed in either uppercase or lowercase, i.e, the strings `NO`, `no`, `nO`, and `No` will all be treated as equivalent.
content.inputFormat: The first line contains an integer T (1 ≤ T ≤ 10), the number of test cases. Each of the next T lines contains a single integer N (1 ≤ N ≤ 31), representing today's date in March.
content.outputFormat: For each test case output "Yes" if N equals 15, otherwise output "No". Each answer should be on its own line.

sampleTestCases.explanation: There is one test case with N = 14. Since 14 ≠ 15, the answer is No.
There is one test case with N = 15. This is exactly the ides of March, so the answer is Yes.
Three test cases: 10 → No, 15 → Yes, 31 → No.

solution.approach: Read the number of test cases. For each test case, compare the given day N with the constant 15. If they are equal, the day is the ides of March, so output "Yes"; otherwise output "No". This runs in O(T) time and O(1) extra space.
solution.timeComplexity: O(T) – one constant‑time comparison per test case.
solution.spaceComplexity: O(1) – only a few variables are used regardless of T.
